queue.title: How to build a custom AI harness with Claude SDK
queue.description: Everybody is saying, “It’s not the model, it’s the harness,” but almost nobody stops to explain what a harness actually is. So I did. I built one live on the show: a Sentry bug-debugging harness for my company ChatPRD, using the Claude Agent SDK, a custom terminal UI built with the Ink library, and opinionated adapters for Sentry, Linear, GitHub, and Vercel. The harness handles evidence gathering,
queue.editorial_reason: Live-builds a real Claude Agent SDK harness for bug debugging; concrete, technical, demonstrates actual workflow.

queue.title: YC's Head of Design Shows You How To Design With AI
queue.description:
AI isn't just changing the tools designers use. It's changing the way they think, prototype, and build.

In this episode of Design Review, YC Head of Design Eve Bouffard joins General Partner Aaron Epstein to share the AI-first workflow she uses to design products, websites, and events.

Using projects like Paxel, SOTA Zine, and YC Startup School as examples, she explains how coding agents are tra
queue.editorial_reason: YC's design lead demos a credible AI-first design workflow across real products, not just tool lists.

queue.title: Pydantic AI 2.0: The New Best Way to Build AI Agents is Composing Capabilities
queue.description:
The new best way to build AI agents is to compose capabilities, instead of every agent being a unorganized pile of tools, instructions, and guardrails. That is the shift in Pydantic AI 2.0, and it changes how you build every agent.

A capability is a self-contained brick that bundles its own instructions, tools, model settings, and hooks. You compose an agent by snapping bricks together, and the s
queue.editorial_reason: Cole Medin explains a genuine architectural shift in agent building (composable capabilities) with credible depth.

queue.title: The canvas is not dead ft. Catt Small (Dropbox) | Config 2026
queue.description: You've probably heard it: 'the canvas is dead. Just prompt your way to a UI and ship it.' But Catt doesn't work on simple problems. She designs for tens of millions of users, inside products with years of context and use cases AI tools can't easily handle. When the complexity is that deep, you can't skip the part where you actually think. She'll walk through her framework for deciding what deserve
queue.editorial_reason: Catt Small on designing complex products AI can't easily handle; credible, substantive HCI perspective.

queue.title: Stop Checking for None Everywhere
queue.description:
🧱 Build software that lasts. Join the Software Design Mastery waiting list → https://arjan.codes/mastery.

'None' seems harmless at first. But once it starts spreading through your codebase, every function needs more checks, more validation, and more defensive programming.

In this video, I show several ways to avoid overusing 'None', including better defaults, fail-fast validation, explicit state
queue.editorial_reason: ArjanCodes gives concrete, non-hypey techniques for eliminating None-checking sprawl in real codebases.

queue.title: Stop Adding Lazy AI to Your SaaS
queue.description:
There are only 6 ways to actually use AI as a feature in your SaaS. 

After talking with hundreds of TinySeed companies, that's the number I kept landing on.

In this talk from MicroConf US in Portland, I break down all 6, with real examples from companies you know and some you've never heard of.

🔗 Links in the video:
MicroConf Channel: https://www.youtube.com/@MicroConf 
MicroConf Europe┃Reykjav
queue.editorial_reason: Rob Walling's MicroConf talk gives a concrete framework for AI features in bootstrapped SaaS with real examples.

queue.title: SEE CMUX SOLVE Multi-Agent Orchestration (Claude Code and Pi Agent)
queue.description:
🔥 Hot take: An agent you can't SEE is an agent you can't improve. 

Spinning up 20 agents in a loop and looking the other way isn't agentic engineering, it's  gambling with tokens, its vibe coding... 

🎥 VIDEO REFERENCES

queue.title: I Tested GPT‑5.6 Sol for a Month
queue.description:
Dan Shipper, CEO of Every, spent a month testing OpenAI’s GPT‑5.6 Sol. It is fast, powerful, relatively inexpensive and an excellent writer. 

Dan breaks down how GPT‑5.6 performs across coding, writing, design, and knowledge work, why he compares it to a Porsche, when to use it vs. Fable 5 and Opus 4.8, and how models like this let us move from doing every task ourselves to managing systems that
queue.editorial_reason: Every's month-long hands-on evaluation of a new model gives credible, specific comparative insight.

queue.title: The Only Claude Code Plugins You Actually Need
queue.description:
Add TigerData's MCP to start controlling databases right in Claude! https://tsdb.co/vaezqa19

Everyone tells you to install as many Claude Code plugins and MCP servers as possible. Here's the problem: once Claude can see more than about 50 tools at once, it starts picking the wrong ones - and your agent actually gets worse, not better. In this video I went through the entire plugin marketplace and
queue.editorial_reason: Practical explanation of why fewer, curated MCP tools beat exhaustive plugin lists in Claude Code.

queue.title: Shor's Algorithm for Quantum Computing - Computerphile
queue.description:
We brought a computer scientist and a physicist together to talk about Shor's algorithm - a famous factorisation algorithm for that can break some currently used encryption techniques if implemented in the quantum realm. 

Phil's accompanying blog post: https://muircheartblog.wpcomstaging.com/2026/05/26/quantum-computing-one-universe-is-more-than-enough/ 

Computerphile is supported by Jane Street
queue.editorial_reason: Computerphile gives a genuinely clear, credible explainer on Shor's algorithm and quantum factorization.

queue.title: LGTM, Ship It: The AI Code Review Problem
queue.description: This episode tackles the growing pains of AI-assisted development, from the struggle of reviewing thousands of lines of agent-generated code to the mounting technical debt when teams merge PRs without meaningful human review. Scott and Wes also dig into local models, whether jujutsu really beats git, how freelancers should price work in the AI era, and getting your team on board with external libr
queue.editorial_reason: Syntax hosts a substantive discussion on AI code review pitfalls and technical debt from PR-merging agents.

queue.title: In search of frontier AI at home
queue.description:
Analyzing a handful of local models that fit within a specific GPU memory ceiling, looking to learn how much compression impacts performance, how differently compressed models @ similar size compare, and how kv cache impacts attention in some cases.

Benchmarks writeup: https://hkinsley.com/reflections/in-search-of-the-frontier-at-home
DeepSeek V4 Flash: https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek
queue.editorial_reason: Sentdex runs real local-model benchmarks on GPU memory tradeoffs, genuine research over hype.

queue.title: This MCP Gives Claude Code 600,000+ Real Design References
queue.description:
Get 20% off your Mobbin subscription: https://mobbin.com/griffin #ad  

Claude Code can now pull from over 600,000 real UI screens before it designs anything — real onboarding flows, paywalls, settings pages, and empty states from apps that actually shipped. In this video I connect the Mobbin MCP to Claude Code and walk through exactly how to set it up, how to prompt it so it pulls the right refer
queue.editorial_reason: Concrete demo connecting a real design-reference MCP to Claude Code for grounded UI generation.

queue.title: 🚀Claude Fable 5 太贵怎么办？我实测Claude Code模型分层用法：Advisor、Workflow、OpenSpec都能省大量Token！Anthropic官方亲自推荐的的省钱技巧
queue.description:
🚀视频简介
✅用最少的Fable 5 Token发挥最大威力：Advisor、Dynamic Workflow、OpenSpec三种方案全演示
🔥本期视频分享 Claude Fable 5 在 Claude 订阅中延期下架后，如何更合理地在 Claude Code 中使用它。
Fable 5 很强，适合复杂代码库、系统设计、架构决策、长链路推理和疑难 bug 排查，但它的 token 成本也非常高。
视频中我会演示三种更省额度的用法：让 Fable 5 作为 Advisor 搭配 Sonnet 5 执行任务；在 dynamic workflow 中让不同模型分工协作；以及用 Fable 5 生成 plan 或 OpenSpec 规格文档，再切换到 Opus 4.8 或 Sonnet 5 执行开发。这样既能保留 Fable 5 的高质量判断能力，又能明显降低整体 token 消耗。

👉👉👉
queue.editorial_reason: Practical, specific breakdown of model-tiering strategies to cut Claude Code token costs.

pool.title: I Love the Karpathy LLM Wiki but it Doesn't Scale. Here's What Does.
pool.description:
Markdown makes great memory for an AI agent - a Karpathy-style LLM wiki, an Obsidian second brain it reads and writes. If the agent is just yours, a folder of markdown is hard to beat: simple, yours, all in git. My second brain is exactly that!

It falls apart the second you ship that agent to other people. Now the memory has many users at once, the data behind it changes in real time, and you nee
pool.editorial_reason: Thoughtful analysis of scaling agent memory beyond markdown wikis, from a credible builder-educator.

pool.title: How I run autonomous coding agents from my phone with OpenAI Symphony + Linear
pool.description: Alessio Fanelli, founder of Kernel Labs and co-host of Latent Space podcast, walks us through two very different AI workflows: (1) a fully autonomous coding setup using OpenAI Symphony + Linear, where Linear acts as a state machine and Symphony manages agents through the whole dev lifecycle with zero babysitting; (2) Codex with browser access searching eBay for underpriced Pokémon cards—autonomous
pool.editorial_reason: Detailed walkthrough of an autonomous multi-agent coding setup from a credible builder, real specifics.

pool.title: Best practices are trapped in your head and your AI has no idea ft. Harald Kirschner | Config 2026
pool.description: Your tone of voice, your component principles, your definition of "done"—this is the taste you've built over years. Right now it lives in your head, in scattered docs, or in places your tools can't see. Every AI session starts from scratch because that context is invisible. This talk shows how to connect those dots: building a shared knowledge layer so AI can help with specs, prototypes, and desig
pool.editorial_reason: Practical talk on capturing tacit design taste as reusable AI context, directly useful for small teams.

pool.title: Dot Plots: How to Actually See What Your Users Are Doing
pool.description: One of the biggest mistakes founders make is relying on aggregate user metrics instead of understanding how any individuals use their product. In this episode of Startup School, YC General Partner David Lieb walks through one of his favorite tools for better understanding your users, the dot plot. It’s a simple two-dimensional grid that maps individual user activity over time and it can help separ
pool.editorial_reason: YC partner explains a concrete, underused product analytics technique (dot plots) with real reasoning.

pool.title: Beyond design systems: designing for robots and seniors ft. Anna Oh (Norbert Health) | Config 2026
pool.description: While AI reshapes the world, many who need it most are left behind. At Norbert Health, Anna Oh designs robotic AI for seniors with dementia and limited mobility in nursing homes. Working directly with real patients and nurses, Oh and her team built human systems that help robots listen, respond, and comfort.This talk explores what patients taught Anna about embodied AI, and why design isn’t just a
pool.editorial_reason: Specific, credible accessibility-focused HCI case study designing robots for seniors with dementia.

pool.title: Prototyping for the physical world ft. Frederik Ueberschär & Sam Thorne | Config 2026
pool.description: What if Figma’s canvas could extend into the physical world? At Electrolux, we use Figma to design the next generation of home appliances. This talk shares how we bridged the digital-physical gap by building "Figma Displays" with off-the-shelf electronics to live-stream prototypes onto production-accurate screens. Learn how this new process shrunk our feedback loop from six months to just two minu
pool.editorial_reason: Novel hardware-bridging prototyping technique (Figma Displays) with concrete implementation details.

pool.description:
Noah spends many days at Figma prototyping janky interactions, and other days perfecting editor microinteractions so subtle they almost go unnoticed. How do these two engineering mindsets interact? pool.editorial_reason: Figma engineer reflects on craft tension between janky prototyping and polished microinteractions.

pool.description:

Uncovering a dozen nuggets of gold you may have missed from all the viral headlines, I can also assure you you’ll learn something you didn’t know before.

pool.editorial_reason: Credible analytical roundup surfacing specific model details across several new releases, not just hype.

pool.title: From handoffs to upstream ft. Frederick Andersen (EDL) | Config 2026
pool.description: A year ago, EDL made a bet. Not on a tool or a trend, but on a person and a different idea of what a design studio could own. Today, EDL pushes to clients' Git repos, runs their design systems in code, and sits upstream of their engineering teams. This wasn't accidental. It was intentional. This is the full story: the moment the team saw the opportunity, the rituals that made it real, the metrics
pool.editorial_reason: Concrete case study of a design studio moving design systems into code/git, relevant workflow shift.

pool.title: Scooter, screen and duct-tape: from hack to habit ft. Unni K. Manikoth & Harish Kumar | Config 2026
pool.description:
What if your next great design tool doesn’t exist yet? At Ather Energy, designers built their own using Arduinos, iPads, and Figma. This talk unpacks the ways of going outside the design loop, how hacks become habits, and how scrappy experimentation helps teams validate ideas, influence stakeholders, and build magical real-world experiences faster.
_________________________________________________
pool.editorial_reason: Genuine DIY prototyping story building custom hardware tools inside a design workflow.

Recently, Eddie built Gusto Cofounder, a new AI product that automates recurring business processes end-to-end, all triggered thro
pool.editorial_reason: Gusto co-founder's concrete story building an internal AI cofounder tool, real product decisions.

لو بتستخدم Claude Code أو أي أداة برمجة بالذكاء الاصطناعي، فغالبًا أنت بتحرق توكنز وفلوس أكتر بكتير مما تتخيل!
